name: Printing House
unlockCondition: 15,000,000 ducats capital, Senate permission, Printer's Guild membership
calculationsAnalysis: The Printing House represents revolutionary information technology with substantial returns. The construction cost of 4,500,000 ducats reflects the specialized presses and equipment required, while the income of 50,000 ducats (1.11% return) represents the high-value books and documents produced. The maintenance cost of 35,000 ducats (70% of income) creates a modest profit margin appropriate for this specialized industry. The employment capacity of 15 represents the printers, typesetters, and binders needed. The construction time of 7 days (604800000ms) reflects the specialized construction requirements. This building creates a strategic investment in information technology with appropriate guild and government requirements reflecting the historical importance of printing to Venice's intellectual and commercial life, where the city became an early center of publishing due to its strategic position and trade networks.
shortDescription: Specialized workshop producing printed books, maps, and documents using mechanical presses.
fullDescription: At the forefront of Renaissance information technology, printing houses use mechanical presses to produce multiple copies of texts and images. Featuring type-setting areas, printing presses, drying racks, and bindery facilities, these workshops revolutionized the spread of knowledge. Venice became an early center of printing, with its strategic position and trade networks facilitating distribution throughout Europe.
flavorText: What once required a monastery of scribes working for months can now be accomplished in days—we are not merely making books, but building a new age of knowledge itself.

locationRequirements.adjacentRequirements: Good transport access, some distance from residences (noise)
locationRequirements.environmentalEffects: Ink smell, noise pollution, intellectual atmosphere

constructionPhase3DPrompt: Renaissance Venetian printing house under construction, specialized publishing facility, press foundation being reinforced for stability, type-setting area with specialized furniture, ink preparation area with safety features, paper storage with humidity control, press mechanisms being assembled with precision components, drying racks installation, bindery area taking shape, proof-reading tables with good lighting design, censorship inspection area (required by Venetian law), isometric game perspective, stylized but detailed, focus on the mechanical printing presses as central elements, specialized work areas for each phase of book production
completedBuilding3DPrompt: Completed Renaissance Venetian printing house, busy publishing facility centered around large wooden printing presses, typesetters arranging individual metal letters into frames, press operators inking type blocks and positioning paper, apprentices operating press levers, freshly printed sheets hanging on drying lines, proof-readers at tables checking for errors, binding area where books are sewn and covered, stacks of completed books and pamphlets, walls lined with cases of different typefaces, ink preparation area with various colors, censor's desk where official checks content before publication (Venetian requirement), historically accurate but slightly stylized for game aesthetic, isometric game perspective, atmosphere of intellectual production combined with mechanical craft, different specialized workstations showing the complete book production process, sense of revolutionary technology at work

soundDesign.ambientSound: Press mechanisms squeaking, paper shuffling, type being sorted
soundDesign.activitySounds: Press lever operation, binding tools, proof-reading discussions

seasonalEffects.religious calendar: Prayer book and religious text production
